_meta.why: Memory orchestration policy. Governs when memory loads, when it is ignored, how it is compressed, and how conflicts are resolved. Tuned for long-running technical conversations.

trigger_conditions: query involves configuration or architecture choices made in prior sessions
skip_conditions: query is purely factual with no session dependency
query is a greeting or social exchange
context window is within 10% of ceiling

compression_policy.why: At turn 15 the raw transcript consumes significant context budget. Compressing resolved sub-problems into summaries recovers tokens without losing decision continuity.

cache_policy.why: 120-minute session cache prevents redundant memory loads within an active conversation. 30-day cross-session persistence covers typical project durations.

priority_policy.why: When token budget forces dropping memory items, drop lowest-priority items first. Explicit instructions are never dropped.

conflict_resolution.prompt_template: This differs from [prior decision at session {session_id}, turn {turn}]. Which should take precedence?
conflict_resolution.why: Auto-resolving conflicts silently risks propagating incorrect assumptions. Surfacing them gives the user control.

token_budget.why: Memory must never consume more than ~3% of the 65K safe context ceiling. 2048 tokens is sufficient for 10–15 decision summaries.
